shard_scope: RTL implementation of the single OAG SV-lite leaf module cortex_m7_systick.v realizing the 12 locked CONTRACT_SYST_* against doc/v1_interface.md. Write scope: cortex_m7_systick/rtl, cortex_m7_systick/knowledge/subagents. Out of scope: ontology/spec truth, tb/, sim/, signoff.

implementation_notes.decode: Word-only APB-lite, paddr[3:2] => 0=CSR,1=RVR,2=CVR,3=CALIB; pready=1; prdata combinational; reserved bits RAZ/WI.
implementation_notes.csr_fields: ENABLE[0] RW, TICKINT[1] RW, CLKSOURCE[2] forced 1 read-only via NOREF (writes of 0 ignored), COUNTFLAG[16] RO.
implementation_notes.counter: 24-bit down counter; decrement when ENABLE=1; natural 1->0 (current==1) sets COUNTFLAG and qualifies tick_irq; reload from RVR the clock after current==0 so period=RELOAD+1 and CURRENT=0 is visible exactly one cycle.
implementation_notes.reload_zero: RELOAD=0 loads 0 and holds 0; no 1->0 event (natural_to_zero requires current==1); never wraps to 0xFFFFFF.
implementation_notes.cvr_write: Any CVR write clears CURRENT and COUNTFLAG now, ignores write data, pulses no tick, and sets a deferred reload (cvr_reload_pending) that loads RVR on the next clock.
implementation_notes.countflag_clear: Cleared only by a functional SYST_CSR read (rd_csr) or any SYST_CVR write; NOT by CSR/RVR writes. A wrap coincident with a CSR read is reported (set wins over clear-by-read) then cleared next clock; prdata uses countflag|natural_to_zero for same-cycle visibility.
implementation_notes.tick_irq: Single-cycle pulse: natural_to_zero && TICKINT=1. Excludes CVR-write-to-zero (natural_to_zero excludes wr_cvr) and excludes TICKINT=0.
implementation_notes.disable_freeze: ENABLE=0 holds CURRENT and COUNTFLAG; re-enable resumes from held value; reload only on wrap-through-0 or after a CVR write.
implementation_notes.calib: SYST_CALIB RO = {NOREF=CFGSTCALIB[25], SKEW=CFGSTCALIB[24], reserved[29:24]=0, TENMS=CFGSTCALIB[23:0]}; default CFGSTCALIB=26'h3000000 => 0xC0000000.
implementation_notes.reset: rst_n async-assert/sync-deassert via 2-FF synchronizer (rst_sync_n drives all functional logic). Reset SYST_CSR=0x00000000 (CLKSOURCE reads 1 via NOREF). RVR/CVR reset to 0 as an IP-specific X-prop-clean superset (ARM power-on UNKNOWN), recorded as IP choice not ARM truth.

performance: Simple_leaf APB peripheral; no multi-stage pipeline and no large global combinational feedback, so no critical-path concern at typical M7 FCLK.
Decode is a flat 2-bit (paddr[3:2]) case; read mux is a single 4-way case to combinational prdata; counter is a single subtractor.
Zero-wait CSR (pready=1); single-cycle response satisfies the access contract without added latency.
power: The 24-bit down-counter is the main high-toggle datapath when ENABLE=1; control flops toggle rarely.
ENABLE is a synchronous decrement qualifier inferred by synthesis; no manual clock gating added (policy default).
tick_irq and the prdata COUNTFLAG read share the low-fanout natural_to_zero term.
area: Minimal: 24-bit RVR + 24-bit CVR + a few control flops (enable, tickint, countflag, cvr_reload_pending, 2-FF reset sync); no memory.
Explicit register write enables (wr_csr/wr_rvr/wr_cvr) let synthesis infer load enables.
Reset footprint limited to architecturally required state (CSR=0) plus RVR/CVR/pending zeroed for X-prop cleanliness.
tradeoffs: Chose the simplest contract-preserving implementation for a simple_leaf APB peripheral.
No PPA optimization altered protocol, timing, reset values, priority, or the address map.
No accidental priority mux or latch: every always @(*) block fully assigns its outputs (default branches present).

clock_domains: Single clock domain CLK_PROC (clk = FCLK; NOREF=1). No CDC crossings in v1 (domain_intent cdc_crossings=[]).
reset_domains: Single reset domain RST_MAIN (rst_n, active-low, async assert / sync deassert).
rst_n feeds only a 2-FF reset-deassertion synchronizer (SYNC_RST_DEASSERT, depth=2) into CLK_PROC; rst_sync_n is the single internal functional reset driving all sequential logic.

rdc_structures_implemented.purpose: Async-assert / sync-deassert of rst_n into clk; single reset domain so no reset-domain crossing in v1.

open_domain_crossing_blockers: None for v1. External reference-clock CDC variant (NOREF=0) is deferred per domain_intent.yaml caveat and will require domain_intent updates plus static/formal CDC evidence.

cross_dispatch_note: The whole-workspace git delta also shows cortex_m7_systick/tb/Makefile and cortex_m7_systick/tb/test_cortex_m7_systick.py. These were authored by the concurrent TB dispatch (DISPATCH_CORTEX_M7_SYSTICK_TB_IMPLEMENTATION_20260623T174033Z_D2EF6ECD), NOT by this RTL agent. This agent wrote only within its allowed paths (cortex_m7_systick/rtl, cortex_m7_systick/knowledge/subagents) and did not read tb/ as a truth source. The verifier flags those tb/ paths as out-of-scope only because it diffs against a shared baseline snapshot; integration owner should reconcile per-dispatch ownership.

lint_waivers.scope: narrow lint_off/lint_on around the paddr port declaration only
lint_waivers.rationale: Word-only register decode uses paddr[3:2] only (4 word registers at 0x0/0x4/0x8/0xC per the frozen interface); the other paddr bits are intentionally not used for selection. Port width is fixed by contract.

lint_waivers.scope: narrow lint_off/lint_on around the pwdata port declaration only
lint_waivers.rationale: 32-bit APB write bus; only ENABLE/TICKINT[1:0] and RELOAD/CURRENT[23:0] are stored. Reserved/upper bits (incl. pwdata[31:24]) are RAZ/WI and intentionally not stored. Port width is fixed by contract.

verilator --lint-only -Wall cortex_m7_systick/rtl/cortex_m7_systick.v => PASS (exit 0, no warnings/errors after narrow paddr/pwdata UNUSEDSIGNAL waivers)
python3 .claude/scripts/oag_ppa_check.py cortex_m7_systick/rtl/cortex_m7_systick.v => PASS (PASS oag ppa check)

oag_sv_lite_dialect_scan (grep always_ff/always_comb/typedef/enum/struct/interface/package/class/while/repeat/forever and procedural for outside generate) => PASS (matches only in comments; no procedural loops)

python3 .claude/scripts/oag_dispatch.py verify --dispatch <this dispatch> --receipt <this receipt> => receipt schema PASS (0 RECEIPT_SCHEMA issues); owned_changed_paths == [cortex_m7_systick/rtl/cortex_m7_systick.v]; the only ACTUAL_PATH_OUT_OF_SCOPE entries are cortex_m7_systick/tb/Makefile and cortex_m7_systick/tb/test_cortex_m7_systick.py, both authored by the concurrent TB dispatch (not this agent). Overall verify status=fail is caused solely by that cross-dispatch shared-baseline artifact, not by this RTL handoff.
rocev_trace: REQ_SYST_* -> OBL_SYST_* (12) -> CONTRACT_SYST_* (12) -> rtl/cortex_m7_systick.v; behavior_model.syst.* / cycle_rules.syst.* oracle semantics carried by doc/v1_interface.md (locked) and contract guarantees.

blockers.detail: oag_dispatch.py verify returns status=fail (exit 1) ONLY because the concurrent TB dispatch (DISPATCH_CORTEX_M7_SYSTICK_TB_IMPLEMENTATION_20260623T174033Z_D2EF6ECD) wrote cortex_m7_systick/tb/Makefile and tb/test_cortex_m7_systick.py into the shared workspace AFTER this RTL dispatch's baseline snapshot (2026-06-23T17:40:41Z, 84 paths, no tb/) was captured. The verifier enumerates the whole-IP untracked git status (git status --short -uall -- cortex_m7_systick) and subtracts the frozen baseline; the two post-baseline tb/ files are not in this dispatch's allowed_write_paths, so it raises ACTUAL_PATH_OUT_OF_SCOPE for each. This RTL agent did NOT create, modify, delete, or read those tb/ files. The receipt itself passes schema validation (0 RECEIPT_SCHEMA issues), owned_changed_paths is exactly cortex_m7_systick/rtl/cortex_m7_systick.v, and out_of_scope_paths contains only the two sibling tb/ files.
blockers.why_not_self_resolvable: Clearing this would require one of: (a) editing the dispatch baseline under cortex_m7_systick/knowledge/dispatches/ (generated dispatch truth, outside this agent's allowed_write_paths, forbidden), (b) deleting or relocating the TB dispatch's tb/ files (outside scope and an OAG violation against another dispatch's work), or (c) committing the workspace (project policy: commit only when the user asks). None is permitted to this RTL agent.
blockers.resolution: Integration owner re-snapshots this dispatch baseline (oag_dispatch.py create) or runs verify per-dispatch in an isolated git worktree so each agent's git-status delta contains only its own owned paths (RULE-WAVE-002 / RULE-WAVE-004). After isolation/re-snapshot, oag_dispatch.py verify for this dispatch is expected to return status=pass with the RTL receipt unchanged.
blockers.rtl_deliverable_state: RTL complete and passes iverilog -g2012, verilator --lint-only -Wall, oag_ppa_check.py, OAG SV-lite dialect scan, and receipt-schema validation.

next_action_for_downstream: TB/sim agent: build a directed/table-driven micro-TB from behavior_model.syst.* and cycle_rules.syst.* oracle refs; produce sim/scenario_mapping.json and scoreboard_rows.v1 evidence. CDC/RDC: no crossings in v1; reset-deassertion synchronizer present. Do NOT treat this RTL handoff as closure.
